title: Citrosma poeppigii
intro: Citrosma poeppigii, also known as Citrosma, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating various ailments such as fever, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Citrosma poeppigii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Citrosma include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, bronchitis, and digestive issues.

More research is needed to fully understand the classification of Citrosma poeppigii within the family Rubiaceae.
uses: Citrosma poeppigii, a plant native to the Andean region, has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments. Its leaves and stems are used to make infusions, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to be effective in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and nausea, as well as respiratory problems like bronchitis and asthma. In addition, Citrosma poeppigii is used to treat fever, colds, and skin conditions like eczema and acne. More research is needed to confirm the efficacy of the plant's medicinal properties, but it remains a valuable resource in traditional Andean medicine.
